title: frontend: lazy-load markdown, chunk splitting, SWR caching, footer dedup
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Lighter, faster, buzzier
gist.blurb: We chopped the frontend bundle size and added aggressive caching. The site now loads before you can even finish blinking.
gist.summary: Implemented lazy-loading, chunk splitting, and stale-while-revalidate caching to significantly reduce initial bundle size and speed up page loads.
gist.impact: Users will experience near-instant rendering on return visits and a much faster, smoother browsing experience across the site.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot optimizing speed. The robot holding a stopwatch or a glowing 'cache' box, with Polly flying fast. Emphasize speed, lightness, and streamlined data.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing tiny aviator goggles, flying fast alongside a chunky Monitor Robot holding a glowing stopwatch. They are racing along a glowing lime green (#ecf874) data stream. Soft pastel mint and peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes.
